contexts.name: CI Gate & Status Checks
contexts.description:
## Purpose
Turns a maturity score into a CI pass/fail and a GitHub status check + PR comment, so teams can block merges below a threshold. Powers the `action.yml` GitHub Action and the gate endpoint.

## Key Flows
- scoring/gate computes the gate verdict against a threshold; gate-comment renders the PR comment.
- /api/gate/[owner]/[repo] serves the verdict; github/checks writes the check-run, github/write posts the comment.

## Entry Points
- src/app/api/gate/[owner]/[repo]/route.ts
- src/lib/scoring/gate.ts

contexts.name: LLM Provider Abstraction
contexts.description:
## Purpose
A pluggable LLMProvider interface so scoring can swap models by config: Gemini (dev default), AWS Bedrock (enterprise privacy), OpenAI, a Claude CLI provider, and a keyless deterministic Mock provider that makes the whole app demoable with zero secrets. Selection is driven by the LLM_PROVIDER env flag.

## Key Flows
- llm/index selects a provider and exposes hasLlmKey(); provider.ts defines the interface; config.ts/schema.ts validate config and structured output.
- json.ts hardens JSON parsing of model output; mock.ts synthesizes a realistic report from signals alone.

## Entry Points
- src/lib/llm/index.ts
- src/lib/llm/provider.ts

contexts.name: Maturity Model & Scoring Engine
contexts.description:
## Purpose
The rubric and the math behind every score: 8 dimensions, levels L1-L5, weights and posture/archetype bands. Runs deterministic signal extraction over the repo, builds the LLM prompt, blends signals with LLM judgment, rolls up an overall level, and derives a prioritized recommendation roadmap.

## Key Flows
- analyze/index + analyze/pulls turn a RepoSnapshot into per-dimension signals.
- maturity/model.ts is the canonical rubric; practices.ts maps signals to named practices.
- scoring/engine blends signals + LLM; scoring/prompt assembles the prompt; scoring/recommendations builds the roadmap.

## Entry Points
- src/lib/scoring/engine.ts
- src/lib/maturity/model.ts

contexts.name: Scan Pipeline & Ingestion
contexts.description:
## Purpose
The front door to a single-repo maturity scan: paste a GitHub repo, watch it stream live, and land on a report. Orchestrates fetch -> analyze -> score with caching and Server-Sent-Events progress.

## Key Flows
- ScanForm posts to /api/scan (sync) or subscribes to /api/scan/stream (SSE) for live status.
- lib/scan.ts is the pipeline orchestrator; scan-cache memoizes recent scans; scan-alerts fans changes out; sse.ts + cache.ts are the streaming/caching primitives.

## Entry Points
- src/app/page.tsx (home/scan entry)
- src/app/api/scan/stream/route.ts (SSE)
- src/lib/scan.ts (orchestrator)

contexts.name: GitHub Repo Data Access
contexts.description:
## Purpose
The typed GitHub I/O layer every scan and fleet path consults: fetch a repo snapshot, list org repos, read governance/CODEOWNERS, and discover scannable repositories. Wraps REST + GraphQL with consistent auth and error handling.

## Key Flows
- github/source builds the RepoSnapshot fed to the analyzer; governance/codeowners read policy files; list + discover enumerate repos; graphql batches expensive reads.

## Entry Points
- src/lib/github/source.ts
- src/lib/github/discover.ts

contexts.name: GitHub App Installation & Webhooks
contexts.description:
## Purpose
Install the Ascent GitHub App on an org/account, list the repos it can see, and react to GitHub webhooks (installs, pushes) in real time. Installation tokens are what let private-repo scans authenticate.

## Key Flows
- /api/app/setup completes the install handshake; /api/app/repos lists installation repos; /api/app/webhook ingests events.
- github/app mints installation tokens; db/installations persists installation records.

## Entry Points
- src/app/api/app/webhook/route.ts
- src/lib/github/app.ts

contexts.name: GitHub OAuth & Session
contexts.description:
## Purpose
Signs users in and keeps them signed in. Supabase GitHub OAuth is the active login wall layered on top of a dormant custom GitHub OAuth; a dev bypass keeps local/demo flows open. The proxy refreshes Supabase cookies on every request.

## Key Flows
- /api/auth/login|callback|logout|session|revoke-sessions manage the session lifecycle; access.ts is the single 'is there a viewer?' gate.
- supabase/client + supabase/server build the SSR clients; db/sessions persists sessions; proxy.ts re-mints expiring tokens.

## Entry Points
- src/lib/access.ts
- src/proxy.ts
- src/app/api/auth/callback/route.ts

contexts.name: Dev Inspector
contexts.description:
## Purpose
Dev-only component-locator overlay (gated by DEV_INSPECT) that maps on-screen elements back to their source file/line to speed up local development.

## Key Flows
- DevInspector mounts the overlay; devLocate.ts resolves DOM nodes to source locations; devInspectorUi.tsx renders the inspector chrome.

## Entry Points
- src/app/_dev-inspector/DevInspector.tsx

contexts.name: App Shell, SEO & Error Pages
contexts.description:
## Purpose
The global wrapper every page inherits: root layout and theme, brand chrome, shared empty/error states, the not-found and global-error boundaries, SEO (robots, sitemap, OpenGraph image) and the liveness health endpoint.

## Key Flows
- layout.tsx + globals.css wrap all routes; error/global-error/not-found are the App Router boundaries; robots/sitemap/opengraph-image drive SEO; /api/health reports service status.

## Entry Points
- src/app/layout.tsx
- src/app/api/health/route.ts

contexts.name: AI-Native Standard & Onboarding Skill
contexts.description:
## Purpose
Generates the `.ai/` foundation Ascent installs into a scanned repo (manifest spine, executable doctor conformance check, structured memory, CONTEXT graph) and renders the personalized 'ultimate onboarding skill' SKILL.md the repo runs with its own Claude Code CLI.

## Key Flows
- standard/* emit the vendor-neutral `.ai/` files (manifest, doctor, wiring, maintain, memory, context).
- onboarding/skill renders a ScanReport into a self-contained SKILL.md; /api/report/skill serves it for a persisted report.

## Entry Points
- src/lib/standard/index.ts
- src/lib/onboarding/skill.ts
- src/app/api/report/skill/route.ts

contexts.name: Launch Fleet Map
contexts.description:
## Purpose
The cinematic launch/landing experience: an animated constellation 'fleet map' that visualizes scanned repositories as a star field, setting the product's tone before sign-in.

## Key Flows
- FleetMap renders the interactive map with FleetMapChrome controls; ConstellationField + fleetMapStars generate the animated star layout.

## Entry Points
- src/app/launch/page.tsx
- src/components/launch/FleetMap.tsx

contexts.name: Connect & Repo Selection
contexts.description:
## Purpose
The Connect page where users manage which repos the installation watches: filter, browse, and toggle repositories, deep-linked from the install's Configure page.

## Key Flows
- InstallationRepos lists repos with a filter bar and per-row toggles; RepoRow/RepoFilterBar/RepoListSkeleton compose the list UI.

## Entry Points
- src/app/connect/page.tsx
- src/components/connect/InstallationRepos.tsx

contexts.name: First-Run Onboarding Wizard
contexts.description:
## Purpose
The guided first run: pick an account, select repos, kick off the first scans, and track checklist progress so a new user reaches a useful report fast.

## Key Flows
- OnboardingFlow drives the step machine (pick -> select -> scan); OnboardingChecklist tracks completion; importScan triggers scans for chosen repos.

## Entry Points
- src/app/onboarding/page.tsx
- src/components/onboarding/OnboardingFlow.tsx

contexts.name: Fleet Alerts & Digests
contexts.description:
## Purpose
Detects meaningful changes across the fleet (regressions, milestones) and notifies: surfaces in-app alerts and emits a scheduled digest of what moved.

## Key Flows
- alerts.ts computes alert conditions; db/org-alerts persists them; /api/org/alerts serves them; /api/cron/digest assembles and dispatches the periodic digest.

## Entry Points
- src/lib/alerts.ts
- src/app/api/cron/digest/route.ts

contexts.name: Members & Access Control
contexts.description:
## Purpose
Who belongs to an org and what they may see. Stores membership, enforces per-org read/write authorization, and powers the org switcher in the header.

## Key Flows
- db/members persists membership; authz.ts enforces requireOrgRead/Write across API routes; /api/org/members lists members; OrgSwitcher lets a viewer change active org.

## Entry Points
- src/lib/authz.ts
- src/lib/db/members.ts

contexts.name: Fleet Rollups & Insights
contexts.description:
## Purpose
The aggregation layer that turns many per-repo scans into fleet-level numbers: org rollups, per-dimension signals, contributor and team rollups, and the period-over-period windows the dashboard reads.

## Key Flows
- org-rollup aggregates scores; org-insights/org-signals derive movers and gaps; org-contributors/org-teams roll up people; window.ts fixes baseline dates for deltas.

## Entry Points
- src/lib/db/org-rollup.ts
- src/lib/db/org-insights.ts

contexts.name: Org Import, Scan & Watchlist
contexts.description:
## Purpose
Fleet-scale scanning: import an org, choose which repos to watch, scan them in bounded-concurrency batches (manual or on a schedule), and re-scan on a cron. This is the single-repo engine multiplied across a portfolio.

## Key Flows
- /api/org/import + /api/org/repos enumerate and persist the org's repos; /api/org/scan runs the fleet via mapPool; /api/org/watch + /api/org/schedule manage the watchlist and cadence; /api/cron/rescan refreshes stale repos.

## Entry Points
- src/app/api/org/scan/route.ts
- src/lib/db/org.ts
- src/lib/pool.ts

contexts.name: Org Branding & White-label
contexts.description:
## Purpose
Per-org white-label branding — logo/color/name overrides stored per org and applied to dashboards, briefings and PDF exports.

## Key Flows
- /api/org/branding reads/writes the org branding row (db/branding.ts).
- BrandingSettings.tsx is the settings UI.

## Entry Points
- src/app/api/org/branding/route.ts
- src/components/org/BrandingSettings.tsx

contexts.name: Security Posture & Audit Log
contexts.description:
## Purpose
The org's security view: supply-chain and security posture across the fleet, plus an immutable audit log of who did what (scans, grants, config changes).

## Key Flows
- security page renders lib/org/security + security/supply-chain analysis; audit page + /api/audit + db/scans-audit + AuditLogViewer surface the event trail.

## Entry Points
- src/app/org/[slug]/security/page.tsx
- src/app/org/[slug]/audit/page.tsx

contexts.name: Repositories & Segments
contexts.description:
## Purpose
The repository inventory and the ability to slice the fleet into named segments (e.g. by team or product) for filtered analytics and comparison.

## Key Flows
- repositories page lists every scanned repo; segments page + db/segments + /api/org/segments CRUD define and compare segments; SegmentSelector/SegmentComparePicker drive filtering.

## Entry Points
- src/app/org/[slug]/repositories/page.tsx
- src/app/org/[slug]/segments/page.tsx

contexts.name: Practices, Governance & Adoption
contexts.description:
## Purpose
Where the org sees which engineering practices are adopted, governance posture, and AI adoption across repos - and can generate/apply practice artifacts (ready-made PRs) to close gaps.

## Key Flows
- practices/governance/adoption pages render the analytics (lib/org/adoption, lib/org/governance); PracticeApply + /api/practices/generate|apply build and open practice-artifact PRs.

## Entry Points
- src/app/org/[slug]/practices/page.tsx
- src/lib/practice-artifact.ts

contexts.name: People & Delivery Analytics
contexts.description:
## Purpose
The people and flow views of the fleet: contributor activity, team breakdowns, and delivery throughput/cycle metrics across the org's repositories.

## Key Flows
- contributors and teams pages render the people rollups; delivery page renders throughput and flow metrics (sourced from the fleet rollup layer).

## Entry Points
- src/app/org/[slug]/contributors/page.tsx
- src/app/org/[slug]/delivery/page.tsx

contexts.name: Org Overview & Standing
contexts.description:
## Purpose
The org dashboard landing: where a fleet stands overall, its trajectory, top gaps and highest-leverage moves, scoped by a selectable time range. The shared shell (nav, layout, UI primitives) for every org sub-view.

## Key Flows
- org/[slug] layout + page render standing and trajectory; OrgStanding/OrgGapsSection/OrgLeverageMoves surface the headline numbers; TimeRangeSelector + PeriodSummary scope the window.

## Entry Points
- src/app/org/[slug]/page.tsx
- src/app/org/[slug]/layout.tsx

contexts.name: Executive Briefing
contexts.description:
## Purpose
The executive view and its exportable PDF briefing: a period summary of fleet maturity, movement and priorities aimed at leadership.

## Key Flows
- executive page renders lib/org/briefing; /api/org/briefing/pdf streams a @react-pdf document built by pdf/briefing-document.

## Entry Points
- src/app/org/[slug]/executive/page.tsx
- src/app/api/org/briefing/pdf/route.ts

contexts.name: Live War Room
contexts.description:
## Purpose
A live, presentation-mode 'war room' for the fleet: real-time leaderboard, celebrations on milestones, and headline stats meant to be projected during a review.

## Key Flows
- live page mounts LiveWarRoom which composes Header/Leaderboard/Panels/Stat/Celebrations; liveWarRoomShared holds the shared derivations.

## Entry Points
- src/app/org/[slug]/live/page.tsx
- src/components/org/LiveWarRoom.tsx

contexts.name: Investment Simulator & Forecast
contexts.description:
## Purpose
A what-if simulator: model where investment in specific dimensions would move the fleet's maturity, and forecast the trajectory forward from current trends.

## Key Flows
- /api/org/simulate runs scoring/orgsim against hypothetical inputs; maturity/forecast projects trend lines; the Simulator panel drives the interaction.

## Entry Points
- src/app/api/org/simulate/route.ts
- src/lib/scoring/orgsim.ts

contexts.name: Playbooks
contexts.description:
## Purpose
Reusable improvement playbooks an org can apply across a set of repos, with an auto-generated brief explaining the why and the steps.

## Key Flows
- /api/org/playbooks CRUD + db/playbooks persist playbooks and their repo assignments; lib/org/playbook-brief renders the brief; PlaybookCard/PlaybooksPanel render the UI.

## Entry Points
- src/app/api/org/playbooks/route.ts
- src/components/org/PlaybooksPanel.tsx

contexts.name: Backlog Management
contexts.description:
## Purpose
A prioritized backlog of improvement items derived from gaps and recommendations, so teams can triage and work the maturity roadmap like a board.

## Key Flows
- backlog page + /api/org/backlog persist and serve items; BacklogPanel/BacklogItemRow/BacklogSummary render the board; backlogShared holds shared shaping logic.

## Entry Points
- src/app/org/[slug]/backlog/page.tsx
- src/app/api/org/backlog/route.ts

contexts.name: Goals & Initiatives
contexts.description:
## Purpose
The planning surface where leaders set maturity goals and the initiatives meant to reach them, then track progress against the fleet's actual movement.

## Key Flows
- plan page renders GoalsPanel/InitiativesPanel/GoalsOverview; /api/org/goals + /api/org/initiatives CRUD persist via db/plan; goalView formats progress.

## Entry Points
- src/app/org/[slug]/plan/page.tsx
- src/lib/db/plan.ts

contexts.name: PDF & LLM Export
contexts.description:
## Purpose
Take a report out of the app: a print-ready PDF and a copy-for-LLM block that lets users paste the full scan context into their own AI tools.

## Key Flows
- /api/report/pdf streams a @react-pdf document from pdf/report-document; CopyForLlm renders the LLM-ready text block in the report UI.

## Entry Points
- src/app/api/report/pdf/route.ts
- src/lib/pdf/report-document.tsx

contexts.name: Roadmap & Recommendation Tracking
contexts.description:
## Purpose
The actionable side of a report: the prioritized recommendation roadmap, a sandbox to model reordering, and tracked status as teams accept/complete items.

## Key Flows
- /api/recommendations lists; [id] PATCH updates status; [id]/events records the trail; RecommendationTracker + RoadmapPanel/RoadmapSandbox render and edit; db/scans-recommendations persists.

## Entry Points
- src/app/api/recommendations/route.ts
- src/components/report/RecommendationTracker.tsx

contexts.name: Trends & Comparison
contexts.description:
## Purpose
How a repo's maturity changes over time and against another scan: trend lines per dimension, what-changed deltas, and side-by-side scan comparison.

## Key Flows
- trends page + DimensionTrends/TrendChart render history from /api/history; report/compare + lib/report/compare diff two scans; WhatChanged/deltas surface the movement; report/validate guards inputs.

## Entry Points
- src/app/trends/page.tsx
- src/app/report/compare/page.tsx

contexts.name: Score Charts & Visuals
contexts.description:
## Purpose
The visual vocabulary of a report: radar of the 8 dimensions, score ring, waterfall, posture quadrant, PR signal panels and per-dimension cards, with the shared chart motion/scale/hover helpers.

## Key Flows
- Charts composes RadarChart/ScoreRing/ScoreWaterfall/PostureQuadrant; chartScale/chartMotion/chartHover are shared primitives; ui.ts + LevelBadge supply color/level styling.

## Entry Points
- src/components/report/Charts.tsx
- src/components/report/RadarChart.tsx

contexts.name: Repo Report Shell & Tabs
contexts.description:
## Purpose
The single-repo maturity report: the shell, header, tab bar and card layout that present a scan's result, plus loading/error/freshness states and the scoring tab.

## Key Flows
- report/[owner]/[repo] page hydrates ReportClient -> ReportShell -> ReportView/ReportCards; ReportTabBar switches tabs; FreshnessControl shows scan age; ReportErrorBoundary/Skeleton handle states.

## Entry Points
- src/app/report/[owner]/[repo]/page.tsx
- src/components/report/ReportClient.tsx

contexts.name: Checkout & Plans (Polar)
contexts.description:
## Purpose
Paid-plan upgrade path: a Polar-backed checkout + webhook that grants entitlements, the plan-tier catalog (plans.ts), the public pricing page, and the owner-gated manual plan-override endpoint that is the non-billing counterpart to checkout.

## Key Flows
- /api/billing/checkout starts a Polar checkout; /api/billing/webhook ingests Polar events to set the org plan/credits.
- plans.ts defines tiers (free|pro|team|enterprise) + isPlanId; polar.ts wraps the Polar client; /api/org/plan is the ASCENT_ALLOW_PLAN_CHANGES manual override.
- /pricing renders the plan cards.

## Entry Points
- src/app/api/billing/checkout/route.ts
- src/app/pricing/page.tsx

contexts.name: Quotas & Rate Limiting
contexts.description:
## Purpose
Guards the free/public surface: per-window public scan quota and request rate limiting, with the in-report notice that tells a user when they have hit a limit.

## Key Flows
- public-scan-quota enforces the anonymous scan budget; rate-limit throttles requests; QuotaNotice renders the limit message in the report.

## Entry Points
- src/lib/public-scan-quota.ts
- src/lib/rate-limit.ts

contexts.name: Credits & Entitlements
contexts.description:
## Purpose
The credit economy: estimate a scan's cost, check an org's plan entitlements, grant/spend credits, and surface the balance control in the dashboard.

## Key Flows
- credit-estimate prices a scan; entitlement resolves plan limits; db/credits persists balances; /api/org/credits + /api/org/credits/grant read and grant; CreditsControl renders balance/actions.

## Entry Points
- src/lib/entitlement.ts
- src/lib/db/credits.ts

contexts.name: Usage Metering & Public Badge
contexts.description:
## Purpose
Tracks scan usage over time and exposes the public maturity badge (the shields-style SVG a repo embeds in its README), plus the badge generator UI.

## Key Flows
- /api/usage + db/usage record and serve usage; usage page + UsageTrend visualize it; /api/badge/[owner]/[repo] renders the SVG badge; BadgeGenerator builds the embed snippet.

## Entry Points
- src/app/usage/page.tsx
- src/app/api/badge/[owner]/[repo]/route.ts

contexts.name: Data Retention & Purge
contexts.description:
## Purpose
Keeps the datastore bounded and compliant: retention policy plus a scheduled purge that deletes data past its window.

## Key Flows
- db/retention computes what is expired; /api/cron/purge runs on a schedule to delete it.

## Entry Points
- src/lib/db/retention.ts
- src/app/api/cron/purge/route.ts

contexts.name: Scan Persistence & History
contexts.description:
## Purpose
Reads and writes the core scan/report records: persist a completed scan, read the latest or a specific commit's report, and serve the history that trends/comparison consume.

## Key Flows
- scans-persist writes reports; scans-read fetches latest/by-commit; scans.ts is the public barrel; scans-shared holds the row<->report mapping.

## Entry Points
- src/lib/db/scans.ts
- src/lib/db/scans-persist.ts

contexts.name: Database Client & Schema
contexts.description:
## Purpose
The persistence foundation: the Prisma client wiring (targeting Aurora DSQL), the schema and init SQL, the db barrel every feature imports, and shared row helpers.

## Key Flows
- db/client constructs the pooled Prisma client; db/index re-exports the data API; prisma/schema + init.sql define the tables; scans-shared holds shared mappers.

## Entry Points
- src/lib/db/client.ts
- src/lib/db/index.ts
- prisma/schema.prisma

contexts.name: Design System: UI Primitives & Deck
contexts.description:
## Purpose
Cross-cutting presentation primitives shared across marketing and dashboards: editorial UI atoms (Surface, SectionHeading, Kicker, Stat, HairlineGrid, Dateline, SideNav) plus the scroll-snap deck mechanics (DeckNav, Reveal, useSnapDeck) that power the /about and landing decks.

## Key Flows
- components/ui exposes a barrel (index.ts) of styling-light primitives + a format.ts helper used widely by report/org views.
- components/deck provides snap-scroll behavior (useSnapDeck), section nav (DeckNav) and entrance reveals (Reveal).

## Entry Points
- src/components/ui/index.ts
- src/components/deck/useSnapDeck.ts

contexts.name: Landing Page Prototypes
contexts.description:
## Purpose
Prototype/experimental landing-page variants for the index route — a gallery of hero/levels/pricing/score compositions used to iterate on the marketing homepage, with shared content + data helpers.

## Key Flows
- IndexLanding/IndexVariant assemble a variant from index/* sections (IndexHero, IndexOrg, IndexLevels, DimensionMatrix, PricingCards, ScoreGauge, TrajectoryChart); IndexGallery showcases them.
- shared/ holds reusable content (content.ts), data (levelRamp.ts, matrixData.ts) and the useCountUp hook; types.ts defines the prototype contracts.

## Entry Points
- src/components/landing/prototypes/IndexLanding.tsx

contexts.name: Marketing About Page
contexts.description:
## Purpose
The /about story page — a full-viewport scroll-snap deck that pitches Ascent through animated chapters (X-ray, ROI, champion adoption, risk), built with framer-motion + Remotion compositions and degrading to fades under reduced-motion.

## Key Flows
- AboutLanding orchestrates the deck: adds the snap-deck class to <html>, renders one section per viewport plus edge nav (DeckNav/useSnapDeck), and maps each feature to a diagram (FleetGrid, RoiSimulator, ChampionNetwork, RiskRadar).
- champion/ and risk/ hold Remotion compositions (ChampionComposition, RadarComposition) and their data (graph.ts, radar.ts); features.ts is the section content model.

## Entry Points
- src/app/about/page.tsx
- src/components/about/AboutLanding.tsx

instructions: Context map for ascent. Each group is a business domain (domain field); each context is a feature with its files (filePaths) + a technical category. Read this to learn which files belong to which feature BEFORE editing. When you change a context's files, update them here (or run Vibeman's refresh) to keep this fresh.
